page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical CPU cores with 8 vCPUs, operating at a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This 1:2 CPU-to-memory ratio positions the server within compute-oriented configurations where processing power is prioritized over large memory footprints.

The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance type.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is configured for workloads that require consistent processing power without massive memory demands. Suitable workloads include web serving, lightweight application servers, build systems, and distributed compute nodes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-oriented virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system includes 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. This instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation makes it a reliable option for processing-focused workloads. This hardware profile is suited for web serving, application hosting, and general-purpose compute tasks that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high memory capacity.
